req.title: Cheque deposit by mobile (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall deposit a cheque via mobile image; cheques above $5,000 shall require manual verification. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-044 defines two decision branches (auto-approve ≤$5,000 vs manual-queue >$5,000) plus an error path for bad MICR images. BVA demands tests at $4,999.99, $5,000.00, and $5,000.01. EP covers a normal mid-range deposit. NEG covers the 422 MICR-failure path. DT covers the combined (amount × image-quality) decision space. STC verifies the state transition from 'image-uploaded' to 'deposit-pending' then 'decisioning-queued'. Five tests are provided: one EP positive, one NEG, and three boundary/decision variants at the exact threshold edges.

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_below_threshold_auto_approved
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid session token
A valid cheque image with decodable MICR line is available
Cheque amount is $2,500.00 (mid-range, well below $5,000 threshold)
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image and amount=2500.00
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-044: cheques at or below $5,000 do not require manual verification
EP: amount 2500.00 belongs to the auto-approve equivalence class [0, 5000]
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_below_threshold_auto_approved(
    auth_session,        # <fixture> returns authenticated requests.Session
    valid_cheque_image,  # <fixture> returns bytes of a MICR-valid cheque image
    api_base_url,        # <fixture> returns base URL string
):
    payload = {
        "amount": "2500.00",
        "currency": "USD",
    }
    files = {"image": ("cheque.jpg", valid_cheque_image, "image/jpeg")}

    resp = auth_session.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/deposits/mobile-cheque",
        data=payload,
        files=files,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 202, f"Expected 202, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "deposit_pending"
    assert body["decisioning_required"] is False
    assert "deposit_id" in body and body["deposit_id"]

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_invalid_micr_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid session token
A cheque image with an undecodable / corrupt MICR line is available
Cheque amount is $1,000.00 (valid amount, isolated image-quality as the variable)
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with corrupt MICR image and amount=1000.00
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-044 error pathway: 422 when image fails MICR decode
NEG: deliberately invalid input to exercise the error branch

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_at_exact_threshold_5000_no_manual_review
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid session token
A valid cheque image with decodable MICR line is available
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image and amount=5000.00
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-044: cheques ABOVE $5,000 require manual verification; $5,000.00 is not above $5,000
BVA: on-point boundary at $5,000.00 must NOT trigger manual review
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_at_exact_threshold_5000_no_manual_review(
    auth_session,        # <fixture> returns authenticated requests.Session
    valid_cheque_image,  # <fixture> returns bytes of a MICR-valid cheque image
    api_base_url,        # <fixture> returns base URL string
):
    payload = {
        "amount": "5000.00",
        "currency": "USD",
    }
    files = {"image": ("cheque.jpg", valid_cheque_image, "image/jpeg")}

    resp = auth_session.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/deposits/mobile-cheque",
        data=payload,
        files=files,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 202, f"Expected 202, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "deposit_pending"
    assert body["decisioning_required"] is False, \
        "$5,000.00 must NOT trigger manual review (rule is strictly >5000)"
    assert "deposit_id" in body and body["deposit_id"]

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_one_cent_above_threshold_queues_manual_review
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid session token
A valid cheque image with decodable MICR line is available
Cheque amount is $5,000.01 (off-point boundary, one cent above threshold)
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image and amount=5000.01
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: response body contains decisioning_queue non-empty string
trace: R-FIN-044: cheques ABOVE $5,000 require manual verification
BVA: off-point at $5,000.01 must trigger manual review and decisioning queue
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_one_cent_above_threshold_queues_manual_review(
    auth_session,        # <fixture> returns authenticated requests.Session
    valid_cheque_image,  # <fixture> returns bytes of a MICR-valid cheque image
    api_base_url,        # <fixture> returns base URL string
):
    payload = {
        "amount": "5000.01",
        "currency": "USD",
    }
    files = {"image": ("cheque.jpg", valid_cheque_image, "image/jpeg")}

    resp = auth_session.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/deposits/mobile-cheque",
        data=payload,
        files=files,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 202, f"Expected 202, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "deposit_pending"
    assert body["decisioning_required"] is True, \
        "$5,000.01 must trigger manual review"
    assert body.get("decisioning_queue"), "decisioning_queue must be populated"
    assert "deposit_id" in body and body["deposit_id"]

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_decision_table_amount_and_image_quality_matrix
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid session token
Both a valid MICR image and an invalid MICR image fixture are available
Test amounts cover below-threshold (4999.99) and above-threshold (5000.01) partitions
actions: Execute four combinations: (valid image, 4999.99), (valid image, 5000.01), (invalid image, 4999.99), (invalid image, 5000.01)
Assert outcome for each combination according to the decision table
trace: R-FIN-044: image quality and amount are independent decision variables
DT: exhaustive 2x2 decision table covering all condition combinations
